summary: High‑energy Oakland show with scorching Scarlet>Fire and masterful keyboards
blurb: The second set rocks a blistering Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain segue and a creative drum jam that fuels the Other One suite. Vince’s organ work shines, especially on Walkin' Blues and the Masterpiece. Overall the band stays tight and upbeat from start to finish.
review: Feb 20, 1991 at the Oakland‑Alameda County Coliseum captured one of the Dead's most electrifying early‑’90 nights. The first set kicked off with a fiery Sugaree that set the tone for a high‑octane run through Walkin' Blues, Friend Of The Devil and a rousing Bird Song. The highlight was undoubtedly the seamless Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain transition, which rolled into an inspired Estimated Prophet and a thunderous drum/space jam that propelled a soaring Other One suite. Vince Welnick’s keyboards were “amazingly right on,” adding organ texture to Walkin' Blues and driving the dynamics of When I Paint My Masterpiece. Jerry Garcia delivered his trademark melodic lead with perfect tone, while the dual drummers kept the groove funky and adventurous throughout. The show ends on a joyful note with The Mighty Quinn, leaving fans with a sense that the band was truly locked in and feeding off the crowd’s energy.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Both recordings are described with positive language – ‘excellent’, ‘stellar’, ‘scorching’, and repeatedly praise the Scarlet>Fire jam, Vince’s keyboards, and the creative drum jams. No negative comments appear, indicating strong consensus on a high‑quality performance.

best_recording.reason: Front‑row audience taper delivers crisp, balanced sound with excellent instrument separation; praised as one of the best‑sounding audience recordings for this show.

key_highlights: Scorching Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain sequence that drives the second set.
Vince Welnick’s keyboard work shines on Walkin' Blues, Masterpiece and throughout the jam sections.
Creative drum/jam/Other One suite keeps energy high and showcases tight interplay between Mickey and Billy.

band_performance.Jerry: Inspired playing with perfect tone; takes melodic forefront and fills the space left by Hornsby.
band_performance.Bob: Starts songs in a high key, mix is improved; vocal levels a bit high but not detrimental.
band_performance.Vince: Receives strong compliments for being ‘amazingly right on’; organ work on Walkin' Blues and Masterpiece is stellar.
band_performance.Mickey: Shows creativity especially during the second‑set drum jam that teases The Other One.
band_performance.Billy: Provides funky, subtle jams that add extra groove to the space sections and keep momentum high.
